title: Beyond GRPO and On-Policy Distillation: An Empirical Sparse-to-Dense Reward Principle for Language-Model Post-Training
core_claim: Sparse sequence-level reward is better spent on stronger teacher models for exploration, then transferred as dense supervision to smaller student models; this sparse-to-dense allocation outperforms direct student-side GRPO in verifiable math post-training.
triage_rationale: The paper has a clear and testable training-data allocation claim, with concrete comparative results across GRPO, distillation, and bridge procedures on math benchmarks. It is methodologically focused and likely useful for readers tracking post-training recipes, though the evidence appears narrow to a specific Qwen3/Llama setup and a single reward-allocation framing.
likely_red_flags: Results may be benchmark- and model-family-specific
Strong empirical framing but abstract does not establish broad generality
Optimization details may matter more than the proposed principle
